Safety Mar 30, 2026

Why Pilots Sometimes Land Hard On Purpose

A harder landing often feels dramatic, but it is frequently the safer landing.

Steep approaches and firm landings reduce exposure to wind shear and runway overrun risks, making aggressive technique tactically superior to smooth descents in challenging conditions.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

American’s BBQ partnership with Pecan Lodge hits the spot

Some airline routes are special because of the insatiable passenger demand they create, like Seoul to Jeju in South Korea, which is often ranked as the world’s busiest route. Others are special because they link two globally influential cities like New York and London. Then there’s Dallas to New...

Airlines increasingly leverage local culinary partnerships to differentiate short regional routes and boost ancillary revenue while enhancing passenger experience beyond traditional in-flight services.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Varda flies navigation payload, heat shield tests on sixth reentry mission

Rhea Space, Sandia and NASA hardware to gather data during high-speed descent...

Varda's reentry missions are becoming a collaborative platform for testing critical hardware from multiple aerospace entities, accelerating development of reusable spacecraft technologies.

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective risk controls require organizations to document why residual risks remain acceptable after mitigation, creating accountability for safety decisions beyond initial hazard identification.

Military Mar 30, 2026

NORAD Escorts Aircraft After Palm Beach TFR Violation

F-16s intercepted the general aviation aircraft near Mar-a-Lago on Sunday afternoon.

TFR enforcement around high-security sites now involves active fighter interception, signaling escalated consequences for airspace violations in sensitive areas.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Sendoff for Artemis II Crew

From left to right, NASA astronauts Andre Douglas, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronauts Jenni Gibbons, NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen pose for a photo before the Artemis II crew proceed to a media event on March 27, 2026. Douglas...

Artemis II's international crew composition—including Canadian Space Agency astronauts alongside NASA personnel—signals NASA's strategic reliance on international partnerships for lunar exploration sustainability.

Only 27% Full: Avelo Airlines' 10 Emptiest Routes Revealed

Discover the shocking low load factors on Avelo Airlines' emptiest routes, but there's a twist...

Avelo's severe underperformance on specific routes suggests either aggressive market entry strategy or potential network optimization failures requiring immediate corrective action.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

How Airline Cabins Have Changed Since 2000

The year 2000 marked a turning point in airline cabin design. How far have these designs come in the years since?

Cabin evolution over two decades reflects shifting passenger expectations and airline cost pressures, directly impacting revenue generation through seat density, premium seating, and ancillary services.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 30, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation safety from incident response to predictive risk identification, reducing accidents before they occur rather than analyzing them after.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Air Algérie orders 10 Boeing 737-8s as fleet renewal accelerates

Air Algérie has ordered 10 Boeing 737 MAX 8 aircraft, with deliveries set to begin in summer 2026 as the…...

North African carriers are committing to Boeing's MAX fleet despite prior safety concerns, signaling restored confidence in the aircraft's market viability.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

The Widebody Boeing Aircraft That Seats More Passengers Than Its Airbus Competitor

This aviation marvel that combines power, efficiency, and record-breaking seating in one sleek package.

Boeing's widebody advantage in maximum density configurations offers airlines higher revenue-per-flight potential, though operational efficiency and real-world seating economics often differ significantly from theoretical maximums.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Inside The Boeing 737 MAX's Flight Control System

How pilots fly the Boeing 737 MAX.

Understanding the 737 MAX's flight control architecture is essential for pilots to recognize how MCAS automation differs from previous 737 variants and manual intervention protocols.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

ESA launches first Celeste satellites to test complementary LEO navigation layer

MILAN — The European Space Agency has launched the first two satellites of the Celeste in-orbit demonstration mission from New Zealand aboard a Rocket Lab Electron launcher on March 28. The two satellites were launched at 10:14 CET and separated from the launcher about an hour later into a...

Europe's Celeste LEO constellation could provide GPS backup for aviation if successful, reducing operational dependency on single-source satellite navigation systems.

See SpaceNews →

▶
Safety Mar 30, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike accidents result from pilots' cognitive overestimation of reaction time, making complacency rather than mechanical failure the primary risk factor in rotorcraft operations.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

SpaceX launches Transporter-16 rideshare mission

SpaceX launched the latest in its series of dedicated rideshare missions March 30, delivering more than 100 payloads to sun-synchronous orbit.

SpaceX's rideshare model demonstrates commercial viability of bundled smallsat launches, lowering barriers for emerging space operators and reducing individual mission costs.

See SpaceNews →

General Aviation Mar 30, 2026

Picture of the Day: March 30, 2026

Formation flying with a friend's Subaru-powered home built.

Subaru auto-engine conversions remain a cost-effective alternative for experimental aircraft builders seeking reliable powerplants without aviation-industry markup.

Military Mar 30, 2026

5 Largest Aircraft Fleets Operated By The US Air Force

From F-16 fighters to KC-135 tankers, these five aircraft types make up the largest fleets in the US Air Force in 2026.

The USAF's fleet composition reveals aging tanker infrastructure, with KC-135s still dominating despite modernization efforts that could impact future operational readiness.

See Simple Flying →

eVTOL Mar 30, 2026

Merlin Labs sees incremental path to pilot-free passenger flights

US start-up Merlin Labs is eyeing civil applications for its AI-powered, defense-grade Merlin Pilot autonomous flight control system and says there is “no hard ceiling” for the size of aircraft its technology could eventually be installed on. Describing fully uncrewed civil flight as “a very long...

Merlin Labs' defense-derived autonomous system could eventually scale to large aircraft, positioning AI-assisted flight as a gradual regulatory pathway rather than a revolutionary leap.

See Runway Girl Network →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

KSQL Noise Abatement Departure

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's constrained terrain and proximity to SFO Class B airspace create unique noise abatement challenges requiring specialized departure procedures for operators.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Spain Bars U.S. Iran-Related Military Flights From Its Airspace

Madrid expands earlier restrictions on base access for U.S. military flights.

Spain's escalating airspace restrictions signal broader European pushback against U.S. Middle East military operations, creating routing challenges for transatlantic military airlift.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

China’s Kinetica-2 rocket debuts successfully, sending prototype cargo spacecraft to orbit

Chinese firm CAS Space successfully reached orbit with the first launch of its Kinetica-2 launch vehicle Monday, with a prototype cargo spacecraft among the payloads.

China's emergence in reliable orbital cargo delivery represents a significant competitive shift in commercial spaceflight capabilities beyond traditional launch markets.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Airbus Faces Persistent Engine Shortages Threatening Future Aircraft Deliveries

The European planemaker is gearing up for its AGM next month.

Engine supply chain constraints are now a critical bottleneck for Airbus's delivery schedule, potentially impacting revenue recognition and customer satisfaction beyond aircraft production capacity.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Ukraine’s private air defense scores first drone kills over Kharkiv

Ukraine’s experimental effort to bring private industry into air defense appears to have reached operational use, with one…...

Private sector air defense integration demonstrates how commercial aviation expertise can rapidly transition to critical national defense infrastructure outside traditional military procurement cycles.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 30, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Integrated optimization across airline, airport, and flight operations—not single-sector solutions—offers the most viable path to balancing capacity growth with environmental sustainability.

See MDPI →

Regulatory Mar 30, 2026

Traffic Resumes After Potomac TRACON Evacuation

Ground stop affected Washington-area airports Friday for the second time in two weeks.

Repeated TRACON evacuations in high-traffic airspace signal potential systemic facility issues requiring urgent FAA investigation to prevent cascading disruptions.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Press Release: The New Terminal One at JFK selects PRA services vendor

The New Terminal One at JFK has selected Wilson James, a leading provider of Passengers Requiring Assistance (PRA) services, as its dedicated partner to design and operate a comprehensive assistance program for passengers who require additional support when traveling through the new...

New Terminal One's PRA vendor selection signals a competitive shift in airport service operations as specialized providers increasingly handle accessibility support rather than airlines managing it directly.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Emirates Pulls The Airbus A380 From 15 Major Routes: Here's Why

The continuing war in Iran has led to a drastic reduction in Emirates' A380 departures from Dubai, with plans subject to rapid change.

Geopolitical instability is forcing airlines to reassess aircraft deployment strategies, with larger aircraft becoming economically unviable on affected regional routes.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Bomb Threat Holds Up Frontier Flight in Atlanta

FBI says there is no continuing threat after passenger’s verbal bomb-threat.

Heightened security protocols and passenger screening procedures continue to create operational disruptions despite low-threat assessments, impacting airline punctuality and customer experience.

Pilatus to provide 12 PC-24s to Indonesian Ministry of Defense contractor

Switzerland’s Pilatus has formed an agreement with Indonesia’s Ministry of Defense (MoD) to provide 12 PC-24 aircraft to…...

Pilatus's PC-24 contract signals growing military adoption of civilian business jets for transport and special operations in Southeast Asia.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

180 Liters Of Water: The Unusual Test Powering The New Airbus A350F

Another key milestone for the forthcoming widebody freighter.

Airbus's water ingestion testing simulates extreme operational conditions that freighter operators may encounter in remote or poorly maintained airports, validating the A350F's reliability in demanding cargo environments.

Military Mar 30, 2026

Airbus’ uncrewed Bird of Prey interceptor autonomously engages kamikaze drone with Frankenburg missile

The Airbus ‘Bird of Prey’ interceptor drone successfully completed its first demonstration flight at a...

Airbus demonstrated autonomous air-to-air combat capabilities, signaling a shift toward AI-driven defensive systems against emerging unmanned threats in military operations.

Military Mar 30, 2026

Spain Continues to Deny Airspace to U.S. Aircraft Involved with Operation Epic Fury

Spanish newspaper El País reported further on earlier news regarding Spain’s position on U.S. military action against Iran, with the country’s Prime Minister confirming all related flight plans have been rejected. After a mass exodus of U.S. Air Force (USAF) aerial refueling aircraft from Spanish...

Spain's airspace denial to U.S. refueling aircraft demonstrates how geopolitical disagreements can operationally constrain military logistics and force routing adjustments.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Press Release: Tigerair Taiwan taps Bluebox wireless IFE for A320 fleet

Bluebox Aviation Systems today announces a new partnership with Tigerair Taiwan to introduce a wireless inflight entertainment experience across the airline’s 17 Airbus A320 aircraft. The deployment will be powered by Bluebox Wow, battery-powered portable wireless system, delivering the Blueview...

Tigerair Taiwan's adoption of wireless IFE eliminates costly seatback installation, reducing aircraft downtime and maintenance complexity while improving passenger experience across its narrow-body fleet.

See Runway Girl Network →

Primoco UAV SE has successfully completed the delivery of Primoco One 150 unmanned aircraft to...

European law enforcement adoption of tactical drones signals growing institutional confidence in unmanned systems for border security and surveillance operations.

Skyports Drone Services (Skyports), the global leader in drone delivery and inspection services, has partnered with...

Remote BVLOS drone operations across borders demonstrate regulatory maturity enabling European construction firms to reduce survey costs while maintaining real-time pilot oversight from distant locations.
